exploits/windows/wins/ms04_045_wins.name: MS04-045 Microsoft WINS Service Memory Overwrite
exploits/windows/wins/ms04_045_wins.description:
This module exploits an arbitrary memory write flaw in the
          WINS service. This exploit has been tested against Windows
          2000 only.
exploits/windows/wins/ms04_045_wins.reason: Targets built-in Windows WINS service for remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/winrm/winrm_script_exec.name: WinRM Script Exec Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/winrm/winrm_script_exec.description:
This module uses valid credentials to login to the WinRM service
          and execute a payload. It has two available methods for payload
          delivery: Powershell 2 (and above) and VBS CmdStager.

          The module will check if Powershell is available, and if so uses
          that method. Otherwise it falls back to the VBS CmdStager which is
          less stealthy.
exploits/windows/winrm/winrm_script_exec.reason: Targets Windows WinRM service and achieves remote code execution with valid credentials.

exploits/windows/vpn/safenet_ike_11.name: SafeNet SoftRemote IKE Service Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vpn/safenet_ike_11.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Safenet SoftRemote IKE IreIKE.exe
          service. When sending a specially crafted udp packet to port 62514 an
          attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code. This module has
          been tested with Juniper NetScreen-Remote 10.8.0 (Build 20) using
          windows/meterpreter/reverse_ord_tcp payloads.
exploits/windows/vpn/safenet_ike_11.reason: Targets Windows VPN software with remote code execution via UDP packet without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/vnc/realvnc_client.name: RealVNC 3.3.7 Client Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/realvnc_client.description: This module exploits a buffer overflow in RealVNC 3.3.7 (vncviewer.exe).
exploits/windows/vnc/realvnc_client.reason: Requires victim to connect with RealVNC client, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_client.name: UltraVNC 1.0.1 Client Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_client.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in UltraVNC Win32
          Viewer 1.0.1 Release.
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_client.reason: Requires victim to connect to attacker server (client-side attack)

exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_viewer_bof.name: UltraVNC 1.0.2 Client (vncviewer.exe) Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_viewer_bof.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in UltraVNC Viewer 1.0.2 Release.

          If a malicious server responds to a client connection indicating a minor
          protocol version of 14 or 16, a 32-bit integer is subsequently read from
          the TCP stream by the client and directly provided as the trusted size for
          further reading from the TCP stream into a 1024-byte character array on
          the stack.
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_viewer_bof.reason: Requires victim to actively connect to malicious server with UltraVNC viewer client.

exploits/windows/vnc/winvnc_http_get.name: WinVNC Web Server GET Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/winvnc_http_get.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the AT&T WinVNC version
          <= v3.3.3r7 web server. When debugging mode with logging is
          enabled (non-default), an overly long GET request can overwrite
          the stack. This exploit does not work well with VNC payloads!
exploits/windows/vnc/winvnc_http_get.reason: Targets Windows NT4 with AT&T WinVNC web server, requires only network access and non-default debug logging enabled.

exploits/windows/unicenter/cam_log_secur.name: CA CAM log_security() Stack Buffer Overflow (Win32)
exploits/windows/unicenter/cam_log_secur.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the CA CAM service
          by passing a long parameter to the log_security() function.
          The CAM service is part of TNG Unicenter. This module has
          been tested on Unicenter v3.1.
exploits/windows/unicenter/cam_log_secur.reason: Targets Windows CA CAM service with remote buffer overflow, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/tftp/attftp_long_filena.name: Allied Telesyn TFTP Server 1.9 Long Filename Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/attftp_long_filena.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in AT-TFTP v1.9, by sending a
          request (get/write) for an overly long file name.
exploits/windows/tftp/attftp_long_filena.reason: Remote buffer overflow in Windows TFTP service requires only network access.

exploits/windows/tftp/distinct_tftp_trav.name: Distinct TFTP 3.10 Writable Directory Traversal Execution
exploits/windows/tftp/distinct_tftp_trav.description:
This module exploits a directory traversal vulnerability in the TFTP
          Server component of Distinct Intranet Servers version 3.10 which
          allows a remote attacker to write arbitrary files to the server file
          system, resulting in code execution under the context of 'SYSTEM'.
          This module has been tested successfully on TFTP Server version 3.10
          on Windows XP SP3 (EN).
exploits/windows/tftp/distinct_tftp_trav.reason: Targets Windows TFTP server software allowing remote file write leading to code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/dlink_long_filenam.name: D-Link TFTP 1.0 Long Filename Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/dlink_long_filenam.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in D-Link TFTP 1.0.
          By sending a request for an overly long file name, an attacker
          could overflow a buffer and execute arbitrary code. For best results,
          use bind payloads with nonx (No NX).
exploits/windows/tftp/dlink_long_filenam.reason: Targets Windows 2000 SP4 via network TFTP service with no user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/futuresoft_transfe.name: FutureSoft TFTP Server 2000 Transfer-Mode Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/futuresoft_transfe.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the FutureSoft TFTP Server
          2000 product. By sending an overly long transfer-mode string, we were able
          to overwrite both the SEH and the saved EIP. A subsequent write-exception
          that will occur allows the transferring of execution to our shellcode
          via the overwritten SEH. This module has been tested against Windows
          2000 Professional and for some reason does not seem to work against
          Windows 2000 Server (could not trigger the overflow at all).
exploits/windows/tftp/futuresoft_transfe.reason: Targets Windows 2000 Pro via network TFTP service with no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/netdecision_tftp_t.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in NetDecision 4.2 TFTP server. The
          software contains a directory traversal vulnerability that allows a remote attacker
          to write arbitrary file to the file system, which results in code  execution under
          the context of user executing the TFTP Server.
targets: NetDecision 4.2 TFTP on Windows XP SP3 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/tftp/netdecision_tftp_t.reason: Exploits a network service vulnerability without user interaction and targets Windows.

exploits/windows/tftp/opentftp_error_cod.name: OpenTFTP SP 1.4 Error Packet Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/opentftp_error_cod.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in OpenTFTP Server SP 1.4.  The vulnerable
          condition triggers when the TFTP opcode is configured as an error packet, the TFTP
          service will then format the message using a sprintf() function, which causes an
          overflow, therefore allowing remote code execution under the context of SYSTEM.

          The offset (to EIP) is specific to how the TFTP was started (as a 'Stand Alone',
          or 'Service').  By default the target is set to 'Service' because that's the default
          configuration during OpenTFTP Server SP 1.4's installation.
exploits/windows/tftp/opentftp_error_cod.reason: Targets Windows with remote code execution via network TFTP service without user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/quick_tftp_pro_mod.name: Quick FTP Pro 2.1 Transfer-Mode Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/quick_tftp_pro_mod.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Quick TFTP Pro server
          product. MS Update KB926436 screws up the opcode address being used in oledlg.dll resulting
          in a DoS.  This is a port of a sploit by Mati "muts" Aharoni.
exploits/windows/tftp/quick_tftp_pro_mod.reason: Targets Windows and achieves remote code execution via network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/tftpd32_long_filen.name: TFTPD32 Long Filename Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpd32_long_filen.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in TFTPD32 version 2.21
          and prior. By sending a request for an overly long file name
          to the tftpd32 server, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and
          execute arbitrary code on the system.
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpd32_long_filen.reason: Remote buffer overflow in TFTPD32 service requires only network access and no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/tftpdwin_long_file.name: TFTPDWIN v0.4.2 Long Filename Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpdwin_long_file.description:
This module exploits the ProSysInfo TFTPDWIN threaded TFTP Server. By sending
          an overly long file name to the tftpd.exe server, the stack can be overwritten.
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpdwin_long_file.reason: Exploits a network service (TFTP) without requiring victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/tftpserver_wrq_bof.name: TFTP Server for Windows 1.4 ST WRQ Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpserver_wrq_bof.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in TFTP Server 1.4 ST.  The flaw
          is due to the way TFTP handles the filename parameter extracted from a WRQ request.
          The server will append the user-supplied filename to TFTP server binary's path
          without any bounds checking, and then attempt to check this path with a fopen().
          Since this isn't a valid file path, fopen() returns null, which allows the
          corrupted data to be used in a strcmp() function, causing an access violation.

          Since the offset is sensitive to how the TFTP server is launched, you must know
          in advance if your victim machine launched the TFTP as a 'Service' or 'Standalone'
          , and then manually select your target accordingly. A successful attempt will lead
          to remote code execution under the context of SYSTEM if run as a service, or
          the user if run as a standalone. A failed attempt will result a denial-of-service.
targets: Windows XP SP2/SP3 EN Service Mode
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpserver_wrq_bof.reason: Targets Windows TFTP service with remote code execution via network without user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/threectftpsvc_long.name: 3CTftpSvc TFTP Long Mode Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/threectftpsvc_long.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in 3CTftpSvc 2.0.1. By
          sending a specially crafted packet with an overly long mode
          field, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code on the system.
exploits/windows/tftp/threectftpsvc_long.reason: TFTP server is third-party software that can be installed and run on Windows for testing.

exploits/windows/telnet/gamsoft_telsrv_u.name: GAMSoft TelSrv 1.5 Username Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/telnet/gamsoft_telsrv_u.description:
This module exploits a username sprintf stack buffer overflow in GAMSoft TelSrv 1.5.
          Other versions may also be affected. The service terminates after exploitation,
          so you only get one chance!
exploits/windows/telnet/gamsoft_telsrv_u.reason: Third-party telnet server with remote buffer overflow that can be installed in a VM.

exploits/windows/telnet/goodtech_telnet.name: GoodTech Telnet Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/telnet/goodtech_telnet.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in GoodTech Systems Telnet Server
          versions prior to 5.0.7. By sending an overly long string, an attacker can
          overwrite the buffer and control program execution.
exploits/windows/telnet/goodtech_telnet.reason: Third-party telnet server software can be installed on Windows VM for remote exploitation.

exploits/windows/ssl/ms04_011_pct.name: MS04-011 Microsoft Private Communications Transport Overflow
exploits/windows/ssl/ms04_011_pct.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the Microsoft
          Windows SSL PCT protocol stack. This code is based on Johnny
          Cyberpunk's THC release and has been tested against Windows
          2000 and Windows XP. To use this module, specify the remote
          port of any SSL service, or the port and protocol of an
          application that uses SSL. The only application protocol
          supported at this time is SMTP. You only have one chance to
          select the correct target, if you are attacking IIS, you may
          want to try one of the other exploits first (WebDAV). If
          WebDAV does not work, this more than likely means that this
          is either Windows 2000 SP4+ or Windows XP (IIS 5.0 vs IIS
          5.1). Using the wrong target may not result in an immediate
          crash of the remote system.
exploits/windows/ssl/ms04_011_pct.reason: Exploits built-in Windows SSL PCT protocol stack without user interaction.

exploits/windows/ssh/freeftpd_key_exchan.name: FreeFTPd 1.0.10 Key Exchange Algorithm String Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/freeftpd_key_exchan.description:
This module exploits a simple stack buffer overflow in FreeFTPd 1.0.10
          This flaw is due to a buffer overflow error when handling a specially
          crafted key exchange algorithm string received from an SSH client.
          This module is based on MC's freesshd_key_exchange exploit.
exploits/windows/ssh/freeftpd_key_exchan.reason: Third-party SSH server software can be installed on a Windows VM and exploited remotely without user interaction.

exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_authbypass.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in FreeSSHd <= 1.2.6 to bypass
          authentication. You just need the username (which defaults to root). The exploit
          has been tested with both password and public key authentication.
exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_authbypass.reason: Targets Windows FreeSSHd service for authentication bypass without user interaction.

exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_key_exchan.name: FreeSSHd 1.0.9 Key Exchange Algorithm String Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_key_exchan.description:
This module exploits a simple stack buffer overflow in FreeSSHd 1.0.9.
          This flaw is due to a buffer overflow error when handling a specially
          crafted key exchange algorithm string received from an SSH client.
exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_key_exchan.reason: Targets Windows with network-based SSH service and no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/ssh/putty_msg_debug.name: PuTTY Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/putty_msg_debug.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the PuTTY SSH client that is
          triggered through a validation error in SSH.c. This vulnerability
          affects versions 0.53 and earlier.
exploits/windows/ssh/putty_msg_debug.reason: Requires victim to actively connect with PuTTY client, making it client-side

exploits/windows/ssh/securecrt_ssh1.name: SecureCRT SSH1 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/securecrt_ssh1.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in SecureCRT <= 4.0
          Beta 2. By sending a vulnerable client an overly long
          SSH1 protocol identifier string, it is possible to execute
          arbitrary code.

          This module has only been tested on SecureCRT 3.4.4.
exploits/windows/ssh/securecrt_ssh1.reason: Requires victim to connect with a vulnerable SSH client, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/ssh/sysax_ssh_username.name: Sysax 5.53 SSH Username Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/sysax_ssh_username.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Sysax's SSH service.  By
          supplying a long username, the SSH server will copy that data on the stack
          without proper bounds checking, therefore allowing remote code execution
          under the context of the user.  Please note that previous versions
          (before 5.53) are also affected by this bug.
targets: Sysax 5.53 on Win XP SP3 / Win2k3 SP0
exploits/windows/ssh/sysax_ssh_username.reason: Targets Windows SSH service with remote code execution via network without user interaction.

extracted_metadata.platform_notes: Windows XP SP3 / Windows Server 2003 SP0

exploits/windows/smtp/mailcarrier_smtp_e.name: TABS MailCarrier v2.51 SMTP EHLO Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/mailcarrier_smtp_e.description:
This module exploits the MailCarrier v2.51 suite SMTP service.
          The stack is overwritten when sending an overly long EHLO command.
exploits/windows/smtp/mailcarrier_smtp_e.reason: Targets Windows SMTP service with remote code execution via network protocol without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/smtp/mercury_cram_md5.name: Mercury Mail SMTP AUTH CRAM-MD5 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/mercury_cram_md5.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Mercury Mail Transport System 4.51.
          By sending a specially crafted argument to the AUTH CRAM-MD5 command, an attacker
          may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/smtp/mercury_cram_md5.reason: Targets Windows SMTP service with no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/smtp/ms03_046_exchange2.name: MS03-046 Exchange 2000 XEXCH50 Heap Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/ms03_046_exchange2.description:
This is an exploit for the Exchange 2000 heap overflow. Due
          to the nature of the vulnerability, this exploit is not very
          reliable. This module has been tested against Exchange 2000
          SP0 and SP3 running a Windows 2000 system patched to SP4. It
          normally takes between one and 100 connection attempts to
          successfully obtain a shell. This exploit is *very* unreliable.
exploits/windows/smtp/ms03_046_exchange2.reason: Targets Windows 2000 Exchange 2000 SMTP service for remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smtp/njstar_smtp_bof.name: NJStar Communicator 3.00 MiniSMTP Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/njstar_smtp_bof.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in NJStar Communicator
          Version 3.00 MiniSMTP server.  The MiniSMTP application can be seen in multiple
          NJStar products, and will continue to run in the background even if the
          software is already shutdown.  According to the vendor's testimonials,
          NJStar software is also used by well known companies such as Siemens, NEC,
          Google, Yahoo, eBay; government agencies such as the FBI, Department of
          Justice (HK); as well as a long list of universities such as Yale, Harvard,
          University of Tokyo, etc.
exploits/windows/smtp/njstar_smtp_bof.reason: Targets Windows SMTP service with remote code execution via network, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/smtp/sysgauge_client_bo.name: SysGauge SMTP Validation Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/sysgauge_client_bo.description:
This module will setup an SMTP server expecting a connection from SysGauge 1.5.18
        via its SMTP server validation. The module sends a malicious response along in the
        220 service ready response and exploits the client, resulting in an unprivileged shell.
exploits/windows/smtp/sysgauge_client_bo.reason: Requires victim to actively connect and validate against attacker SMTP server, making it client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/smtp/wmailserver.name: SoftiaCom WMailserver 1.0 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/wmailserver.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in SoftiaCom WMailserver 1.0
          (SMTP) via a SEH frame overwrite.
exploits/windows/smtp/wmailserver.reason: Third-party Windows SMTP server can be installed in a VM and exploited remotely without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smtp/ypops_overflow1.name: YPOPS 0.6 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smtp/ypops_overflow1.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the YPOPS POP3
          service.

          This is a classic stack buffer overflow for YPOPS version 0.6.
          Possibly Affected version 0.5, 0.4.5.1, 0.4.5. Eip point to
          jmp ebx opcode in ws_32.dll
exploits/windows/smtp/ypops_overflow1.reason: YPOPS is third-party POP3 server software that can be installed on Windows for lab testing.

exploits/windows/smb/cve_2020_0796_smbgh.name: SMBv3 Compression Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/cve_2020_0796_smbgh.description:
A vulnerability exists within the Microsoft Server Message Block 3.1.1 (SMBv3) protocol that can be leveraged to
          execute code on a vulnerable server. This remove exploit implementation leverages this flaw to execute code
          in the context of the kernel, finally yielding a session as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM in spoolsv.exe. Exploitation
          can take a few minutes as the necessary data is gathered.
exploits/windows/smb/cve_2020_0796_smbgh.reason: Targets built-in Windows SMBv3 service on Windows 10 1903-1909 for remote kernel code execution.

exploits/windows/smb/generic_smb_dll_inj.name: Generic DLL Injection From Shared Resource
exploits/windows/smb/generic_smb_dll_inj.description:
This is a general-purpose module for exploiting conditions where a DLL can be loaded
          from a specified SMB share. This module serves payloads as DLLs over an SMB service.
exploits/windows/smb/generic_smb_dll_inj.reason: Targets Windows SMB service for remote DLL injection without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/group_policy_startu.name: Group Policy Script Execution From Shared Resource
exploits/windows/smb/group_policy_startu.description:
This is a general-purpose module for exploiting systems with Windows Group Policy
          configured to load VBS startup/logon scripts from remote locations. This module runs
          a SMB shared resource that will provide a payload through a VBS file. Startup scripts
          will be executed with SYSTEM privileges, while logon scripts will be executed with the
          user privileges. Have into account which the attacker still needs to redirect the
          target traffic to the fake SMB share to exploit it successfully. Please note in some
          cases, it will take 5 to 10 minutes to receive a session.
exploits/windows/smb/group_policy_startu.reason: Targets Windows SMB and provides remote code execution via malicious GPO script without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ipass_pipe_exec.name: IPass Control Pipe Remote Command Execution
exploits/windows/smb/ipass_pipe_exec.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the IPass Client service. This service provides a
          named pipe which can be accessed by the user group BUILTIN\Users. This pipe can be abused
          to force the service to load a DLL from a SMB share.
exploits/windows/smb/ipass_pipe_exec.reason: Targets Windows SMB service with no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/smb/ms03_049_netapi.name: MS03-049 Microsoft Workstation Service NetAddAlternateComputerName Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms03_049_netapi.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the NetApi32 NetAddAlternateComputerName
          function using the Workstation service in Windows XP.
exploits/windows/smb/ms03_049_netapi.reason: Targets Windows XP via SMB/Workstation service, no victim interaction needed.

exploits/windows/smb/ms04_007_killbill.name: MS04-007 Microsoft ASN.1 Library Bitstring Heap Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms04_007_killbill.description:
This is an exploit for a previously undisclosed
          vulnerability in the bit string decoding code in the
          Microsoft ASN.1 library. This vulnerability is not related
          to the bit string vulnerability described in eEye advisory
          AD20040210-2. Both vulnerabilities were fixed in the
          MS04-007 patch.  Windows 2000 SP4 Rollup 1 also patches this
          vulnerability.

          You are only allowed one attempt with this vulnerability. If
          the payload fails to execute, the LSASS system service will
          crash and the target system will automatically reboot itself
          in 60 seconds. If the payload succeeds, the system will no
          longer be able to process authentication requests, denying
          all attempts to login through SMB or at the console. A
          reboot is required to restore proper functioning of an
          exploited system.

          This exploit has been successfully tested with the win32/*/reverse_tcp
          payloads, however a few problems were encountered when using the
          equivalent bind payloads. Your mileage may vary.
exploits/windows/smb/ms04_007_killbill.reason: Targets Windows SMB service with remote code execution and no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/smb/ms04_011_lsass.name: MS04-011 Microsoft LSASS Service DsRolerUpgradeDownlevelServer Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms04_011_lsass.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the LSASS service, this vulnerability
          was originally found by eEye. When re-exploiting a Windows XP system, you will need
          need to run this module twice. DCERPC request fragmentation can be performed by setting
          'FragSize' parameter.
exploits/windows/smb/ms04_011_lsass.reason: Targets built-in Windows LSASS service over SMB for remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms04_031_netdde.name: MS04-031 Microsoft NetDDE Service Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms04_031_netdde.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the NetDDE service, which is the
          precursor to the DCOM interface.  This exploit effects only operating systems
          released prior to Windows XP SP1 (2000 SP4, XP SP0). Despite Microsoft's claim
          that this vulnerability can be exploited without authentication, the NDDEAPI
          pipe is only accessible after successful authentication.
exploits/windows/smb/ms04_031_netdde.reason: Targets Windows 2000 SP4 via SMB/NetDDE service, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/smb/ms05_039_pnp.name: MS05-039 Microsoft Plug and Play Service Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms05_039_pnp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Windows Plug
          and Play service. This vulnerability can be exploited on
          Windows 2000 without a valid user account.

          NOTE: Since the PnP service runs inside the service.exe process, a failed
          exploit attempt will cause the system to automatically reboot.
exploits/windows/smb/ms05_039_pnp.reason: Exploits a built-in Windows service (Plug and Play) remotely without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms06_025_rasmans_re.name: MS06-025 Microsoft RRAS Service RASMAN Registry Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_025_rasmans_re.description:
This module exploits a registry-based stack buffer overflow in the Windows Routing
          and Remote Access Service. Since the service is hosted inside svchost.exe,
          a failed exploit attempt can cause other system services to fail as well.
          A valid username and password is required to exploit this flaw on Windows 2000.
          When attacking XP SP1, the SMBPIPE option needs to be set to 'SRVSVC'.
          Exploiting this flaw involves two distinct steps - creating the registry key
          and then triggering an overwrite based on a read of this key. Once the key is
          created, it cannot be recreated. This means that for any given system, you
          only get one chance to exploit this flaw. Picking the wrong target will require
          a manual removal of the following registry key before you can try again:
          HKEY_USERS\.DEFAULT\Software\Microsoft\RAS Phonebook
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_025_rasmans_re.reason: Targets Windows RRAS service, requires valid credentials but no victim interaction, can achieve remote code execution.

exploits/windows/smb/ms06_025_rras.name: MS06-025 Microsoft RRAS Service Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_025_rras.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Windows Routing and Remote
          Access Service. Since the service is hosted inside svchost.exe, a failed
          exploit attempt can cause other system services to fail as well. A valid
          username and password is required to exploit this flaw on Windows 2000.
          When attacking XP SP1, the SMBPIPE option needs to be set to 'SRVSVC'.
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_025_rras.reason: Targets built-in Windows RRAS service, requires only SMB access and credentials on 2000 or XP SP1.

exploits/windows/smb/ms06_040_netapi.name: MS06-040 Microsoft Server Service NetpwPathCanonicalize Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_040_netapi.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the NetApi32 CanonicalizePathName() function
          using the NetpwPathCanonicalize RPC call in the Server Service. It is likely that
          other RPC calls could be used to exploit this service. This exploit will result in
          a denial of service on Windows XP SP2 or Windows 2003 SP1. A failed exploit attempt
          will likely result in a complete reboot on Windows 2000 and the termination of all
          SMB-related services on Windows XP. The default target for this exploit should succeed
          on Windows NT 4.0, Windows 2000 SP0-SP4+, Windows XP SP0-SP1 and Windows 2003 SP0.
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_040_netapi.reason: Exploits a built-in Windows SMB service without user interaction.

extracted_metadata.platform_notes: Windows NT 4.0, 2000 SP0-SP4, XP SP0-SP1, 2003 SP0

exploits/windows/smb/ms06_066_nwapi.name: MS06-066 Microsoft Services nwapi32.dll Module Exploit
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_066_nwapi.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the svchost service when the netware
          client service is running. This specific vulnerability is in the nwapi32.dll module.
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_066_nwapi.reason: Targets Windows XP SP2 via SMB and requires only the netware client service to be running.

exploits/windows/smb/ms06_066_nwwks.name: MS06-066 Microsoft Services nwwks.dll Module Exploit
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_066_nwwks.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the svchost service, when the netware
          client service is running. This specific vulnerability is in the nwapi32.dll module.
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_066_nwwks.reason: Targets Windows XP SP2 via SMB and requires only the netware client service running.

exploits/windows/smb/ms06_070_wkssvc.name: MS06-070 Microsoft Workstation Service NetpManageIPCConnect Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_070_wkssvc.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the NetApi32 NetpManageIPCConnect
          function using the Workstation service in Windows 2000 SP4 and Windows XP SP2.

          In order to exploit this vulnerability, you must specify the name of a
          valid Windows DOMAIN. It may be possible to satisfy this condition by using
          a custom DNS and LDAP setup, however that method is not covered here.

          Although Windows XP SP2 is vulnerable, Microsoft reports that Administrator
          credentials are required to reach the vulnerable code. Windows XP SP1 only
          requires valid user credentials. Also, testing shows that a machine already
          joined to a domain is not exploitable.
exploits/windows/smb/ms06_070_wkssvc.reason: Exploits built-in Windows Workstation service over SMB without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms07_029_msdns_zone.name: MS07-029 Microsoft DNS RPC Service extractQuotedChar() Overflow (SMB)
exploits/windows/smb/ms07_029_msdns_zone.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the RPC interface
          of the Microsoft DNS service. The vulnerability is triggered
          when a long zone name parameter is supplied that contains
          escaped octal strings. This module is capable of bypassing NX/DEP
          protection on Windows 2003 SP1/SP2. This module exploits the
          RPC service using the \DNSSERVER pipe available via SMB. This
          pipe requires a valid user account to access, so the SMBUSER
          and SMBPASS options must be specified.
exploits/windows/smb/ms07_029_msdns_zone.reason: Targets Windows DNS service via SMB pipe, requires credentials but no victim interaction

exploits/windows/smb/ms08_067_netapi.name: MS08-067 Microsoft Server Service Relative Path Stack Corruption
exploits/windows/smb/ms08_067_netapi.description:
This module exploits a parsing flaw in the path canonicalization code of
          NetAPI32.dll through the Server Service. This module is capable of bypassing
          NX on some operating systems and service packs. The correct target must be
          used to prevent the Server Service (along with a dozen others in the same
          process) from crashing. Windows XP targets seem to handle multiple successful
          exploitation events, but 2003 targets will often crash or hang on subsequent
          attempts. This is just the first version of this module, full support for
          NX bypass on 2003, along with other platforms, is still in development.
exploits/windows/smb/ms08_067_netapi.reason: Exploits built-in Windows SMB service without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms09_050_smb2_negot.name: MS09-050 Microsoft SRV2.SYS SMB Negotiate ProcessID Function Table Dereference
exploits/windows/smb/ms09_050_smb2_negot.description:
This module exploits an out of bounds function table dereference in the SMB
          request validation code of the SRV2.SYS driver included with Windows Vista, Windows 7
          release candidates (not RTM), and Windows 2008 Server prior to R2. Windows Vista
          without SP1 does not seem affected by this flaw.
exploits/windows/smb/ms09_050_smb2_negot.reason: Exploits a remote SMB vulnerability in built-in Windows services without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms10_046_shortcut_i.name: Microsoft Windows Shell LNK Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/ms10_046_shortcut_i.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the handling of Windows
          Shortcut files (.LNK) that contain an icon resource pointing to a
          malicious DLL. This creates an SMB resource to provide the payload
          inside a DLL, and generates a LNK file which must be sent to the
          target.
exploits/windows/smb/ms10_046_shortcut_i.reason: Requires victim to open/click the delivered LNK file

exploits/windows/smb/ms10_061_spoolss.name: MS10-061 Microsoft Print Spooler Service Impersonation Vulnerability
exploits/windows/smb/ms10_061_spoolss.description:
This module exploits the RPC service impersonation vulnerability detailed in
          Microsoft Bulletin MS10-061. By making a specific DCE RPC request to the
          StartDocPrinter procedure, an attacker can impersonate the Printer Spooler service
          to create a file. The working directory at the time is %SystemRoot%\system32.
          An attacker can specify any file name, including directory traversal or full paths.
          By sending WritePrinter requests, an attacker can fully control the content of
          the created file.

          In order to gain code execution, this module writes to a directory used by Windows
          Management Instrumentation (WMI) to deploy applications. This directory (Wbem\Mof)
          is periodically scanned and any new .mof files are processed automatically. This is
          the same technique employed by the Stuxnet code found in the wild.
exploits/windows/smb/ms10_061_spoolss.reason: Exploits a built-in Windows print spooler service over SMB without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms15_020_shortcut_i.name: Microsoft Windows Shell LNK Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/ms15_020_shortcut_i.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the MS10-046 patch to abuse (again) the handling
          of Windows Shortcut files (.LNK) that contain an icon resource pointing to a malicious
          DLL. This creates an SMB resource to provide the payload and the trigger, and generates a
          LNK file which must be sent to the target. This module has been tested successfully on
          Windows 2003 SP2 with MS10-046 installed and Windows 2008 SP2 (32 bits) with MS14-027
          installed.
exploits/windows/smb/ms15_020_shortcut_i.reason: Requires victim to open/click the delivered LNK file, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/smb/ms17_010_eternalblu.name: MS17-010 EternalBlue SMB Remote Windows Kernel Pool Corruption
exploits/windows/smb/ms17_010_eternalblu.description:
This module is a port of the Equation Group ETERNALBLUE exploit, part of
          the FuzzBunch toolkit released by Shadow Brokers.

          There is a buffer overflow memmove operation in Srv!SrvOs2FeaToNt. The size
          is calculated in Srv!SrvOs2FeaListSizeToNt, with mathematical error where a
          DWORD is subtracted into a WORD. The kernel pool is groomed so that overflow
          is well laid-out to overwrite an SMBv1 buffer. Actual RIP hijack is later
          completed in srvnet!SrvNetWskReceiveComplete.

          This exploit, like the original may not trigger 100% of the time, and should be
          run continuously until triggered. It seems like the pool will get hot streaks
          and need a cool down period before the shells rain in again.

          The module will attempt to use Anonymous login, by default, to authenticate to perform the
          exploit. If the user supplies credentials in the SMBUser, SMBPass, and SMBDomain options it will use
          those instead.

          On some systems, this module may cause system instability and crashes, such as a BSOD or
          a reboot. This may be more likely with some payloads.
exploits/windows/smb/ms17_010_eternalblu.reason: Targets built-in Windows SMB service for remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/ms17_010_psexec.name: MS17-010 EternalRomance/EternalSynergy/EternalChampion SMB Remote Windows Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/ms17_010_psexec.description:
This module will exploit SMB with vulnerabilities in MS17-010 to achieve a write-what-where
          primitive. This will then be used to overwrite the connection session information with as an
          Administrator session. From there, the normal psexec payload code execution is done.

          Exploits a type confusion between Transaction and WriteAndX requests and a race condition in
          Transaction requests, as seen in the EternalRomance, EternalChampion, and EternalSynergy
          exploits. This exploit chain is more reliable than the EternalBlue exploit, but requires a
          named pipe.
exploits/windows/smb/ms17_010_psexec.reason: Targets built-in Windows SMB service for remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/netidentity_xtierrp.name: Novell NetIdentity Agent XTIERRPCPIPE Named Pipe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/netidentity_xtierrp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Novell's NetIdentity Agent. When sending
          a specially crafted string to the 'XTIERRPCPIPE' named pipe, an attacker may be
          able to execute arbitrary code. The success of this module is much greater once the
          service has been restarted.
exploits/windows/smb/netidentity_xtierrp.reason: Targets Windows SMB named pipe with no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/smb/psexec.name: Microsoft Windows Authenticated User Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/psexec.description:
This module uses a valid administrator username and password (or
          password hash) to execute an arbitrary payload. This module is similar
          to the "psexec" utility provided by SysInternals. This module is now able
          to clean up after itself. The service created by this tool uses a randomly
          chosen name and description.
exploits/windows/smb/psexec.reason: Requires valid admin credentials but targets built-in Windows SMB service for remote code execution.

exploits/windows/smb/smb_delivery.description:
This module serves payloads via an SMB server and provides commands to retrieve
          and execute the generated payloads. Currently supports DLLs and Powershell.
exploits/windows/smb/smb_delivery.reason: SMB is a built-in Windows service and the module provides network-based payload delivery without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/smb_doublepulsar_rc.name: SMB DOUBLEPULSAR Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/smb_doublepulsar_rc.description:
This module executes a Metasploit payload against the Equation Group's
          DOUBLEPULSAR implant for SMB as popularly deployed by ETERNALBLUE.

          While this module primarily performs code execution against the implant,
          the "Neutralize implant" target allows you to disable the implant.
exploits/windows/smb/smb_doublepulsar_rc.reason: Targets Windows SMB service remotely without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/smb_relay.name: MS08-068 Microsoft Windows SMB Relay Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/smb_relay.description: Use the SMB Connection to run the exploit/windows/psexec module against the relay target
exploits/windows/smb/smb_relay.reason: SMB relay attack can be reproduced in a VM lab by setting up a Windows target and relaying its SMB authentication to another system.

exploits/windows/smb/smb_rras_erraticgop.name: Microsoft Windows RRAS Service MIBEntryGet Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/smb_rras_erraticgop.description:
This module exploits an overflow in the Windows Routing and Remote
          Access Service (RRAS) to execute code as SYSTEM.

          The RRAS DCERPC endpoint is accessible to unauthenticated users via
          SMBv1 browser named pipe on Windows Server 2003 and Windows XP hosts;
          however, this module targets Windows Server 2003 only.

          Since the service is hosted inside svchost.exe, a failed exploit
          attempt can cause other system services to fail as well.

          The module has been successfully tested on:

          Windows Server 2003 SP0 (x86);
          Windows Server 2003 SP1 (x86);
          Windows Server 2003 SP2 (x86); and
          Windows Server 2003 R2 SP2 (x86).
exploits/windows/smb/smb_rras_erraticgop.reason: Targets Windows Server 2003 via SMBv1 named pipe with no user interaction required.

exploits/windows/smb/smb_shadow.name: Microsoft Windows SMB Direct Session Takeover
exploits/windows/smb/smb_shadow.description:
This module will intercept direct SMB authentication requests to
          another host, gaining access to an authenticated SMB session if
          successful. If the connecting user is an administrator and network
          logins are allowed to the target machine, this module will execute an
          arbitrary payload. To exploit this, the target system must try to
          autheticate to another host on the local area network.

          SMB Direct Session takeover is a combination of previous attacks.

          This module is dependent on an external ARP spoofer. The builtin ARP
          spoofer was not providing sufficient host discovery. Bettercap v1.6.2
          was used during the development of this module.

          The original SMB relay attack was first reported by Sir Dystic on March
          31st, 2001 at @lanta.con in Atlanta, Georgia.
exploits/windows/smb/smb_shadow.reason: Targets Windows SMB service and achieves remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/timbuktu_plughntcom.name: Timbuktu PlughNTCommand Named Pipe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/smb/timbuktu_plughntcom.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in Timbuktu Pro version <= 8.6.6
          in a pretty novel way.

          This exploit requires two connections. The first connection is used to leak stack data
          using the buffer overflow to overwrite the nNumberOfBytesToWrite argument. By supplying
          a large value for this argument it is possible to cause Timbuktu to reply to the initial
          request with leaked stack data. Using this data allows for reliable exploitation of the
          buffer overflow vulnerability.

          Props to Infamous41d for helping in finding this exploitation path.

          The second connection utilizes the data from the data leak to accurately exploit
          the stack based buffer overflow vulnerability.

          TODO:
          hdm suggested using meterpreter's migration capability and restarting the process
          for multishot exploitation.
exploits/windows/smb/timbuktu_plughntcom.reason: Targets Windows SMB service with remote code execution via buffer overflow without user interaction.

exploits/windows/smb/webexec.name: WebExec Authenticated User Code Execution
exploits/windows/smb/webexec.description:
This module uses a valid username and password of any level (or
          password hash) to execute an arbitrary payload. This module is similar
          to the "psexec" module, except allows any non-guest account by default.
exploits/windows/smb/webexec.reason: Authenticated SMB-based remote code execution can be reproduced with standard Windows file sharing enabled.

exploits/windows/sip/aim_triton_cseq.name: AIM Triton 1.0.4 CSeq Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/sip/aim_triton_cseq.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in AOL\'s AIM
          Triton 1.0.4. By sending an overly long CSeq value,
          a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code on the system with the privileges of
          the affected application.
exploits/windows/sip/aim_triton_cseq.reason: Exploits a network-exposed service (AIM Triton) without user interaction.

exploits/windows/sip/sipxezphone_cseq.name: SIPfoundry sipXezPhone 0.35a CSeq Field Overflow
exploits/windows/sip/sipxezphone_cseq.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in SIPfoundry's
          sipXezPhone version 0.35a. By sending an long CSeq header,
          a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code on the system with the privileges of
          the affected application.
exploits/windows/sip/sipxezphone_cseq.reason: Targets Windows SIP client software and achieves remote code execution via network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/sip/sipxphone_cseq.name: SIPfoundry sipXphone 2.6.0.27 CSeq Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/sip/sipxphone_cseq.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in SIPfoundry's
          sipXphone 2.6.0.27. By sending an overly long CSeq value,
          a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code on the system with the privileges of
          the affected application.
exploits/windows/sip/sipxphone_cseq.reason: Remote buffer overflow in third-party VoIP client requires no user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/abb_wserver_exec.name: ABB MicroSCADA wserver.exe Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/scada/abb_wserver_exec.description:
This module exploits a remote stack buffer overflow vulnerability in ABB MicroSCADA. The
          issue is due to the handling of unauthenticated EXECUTE operations on the wserver.exe
          component, which allows arbitrary commands. The component is disabled by default, but
          required when a project uses the SCIL function WORKSTATION_CALL.

          This module has been tested successfully on ABB MicroSCADA Pro SYS600 9.3 on
          Windows XP SP3 and Windows 7 SP1.
exploits/windows/scada/abb_wserver_exec.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software (ABB MicroSCADA) and achieves remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/advantech_webacce.description:
This module exploits an arbitrary file upload vulnerability found in Advantech WebAccess 8.0.

          This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on vulnerable installations
          of Advantech WebAccess. Authentication is not required to exploit this vulnerability.

          The specific flaw exists within the WebAccess Dashboard Viewer. Insufficient validation within
          the uploadImageCommon function in the UploadAjaxAction script allows unauthenticated callers to
          upload arbitrary code (instead of an image) to the server, which will then be executed under the
          high-privilege context of the IIS AppPool.
exploits/windows/scada/advantech_webacce.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software (Advantech WebAccess) with remote code execution via file upload, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/scada/advantech_webacce.name: Advantech WebAccess Webvrpcs Service Opcode 80061 Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/advantech_webacce.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Advantech WebAccess 8.2.
          By sending a specially crafted DCERPC request, an attacker could overflow
          the buffer and execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/advantech_webacce.reason: Targets Windows with third-party SCADA software and allows remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/citect_scada_odbc.name: CitectSCADA/CitectFacilities ODBC Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/citect_scada_odbc.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in CitectSCADA's ODBC daemon.
          This has only been tested against Citect v5, v6 and v7.
exploits/windows/scada/citect_scada_odbc.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software (CitectSCADA) with network-accessible ODBC daemon for remote code execution.

exploits/windows/scada/codesys_gateway_s.name: SCADA 3S CoDeSys Gateway Server Directory Traversal
exploits/windows/scada/codesys_gateway_s.description:
This module exploits a directory traversal vulnerability that allows arbitrary
          file creation, which can be used to execute a mof file in order to gain remote
          execution within the SCADA system.
exploits/windows/scada/codesys_gateway_s.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software and achieves remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/codesys_web_serve.name: SCADA 3S CoDeSys CmpWebServer Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/codesys_web_serve.description:
This module exploits a remote stack buffer overflow vulnerability in
          3S-Smart Software Solutions product CoDeSys Scada Web Server Version
          1.1.9.9. This vulnerability affects versions 3.4 SP4 Patch 2 and
          earlier.
targets: CoDeSys v2.3 on Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/scada/codesys_web_serve.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA web server software that can be installed in a VM.

exploits/windows/scada/daq_factory_bof.name: DaqFactory HMI NETB Request Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/daq_factory_bof.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Azeotech's DaqFactory
          product. The specific vulnerability is triggered when sending a specially crafted
          'NETB' request to port 20034. Exploitation of this vulnerability may take a few
          seconds due to the use of egghunter.  This vulnerability was one of the 14
          releases discovered by researcher Luigi Auriemma.
targets: DAQFactory Pro 5.85 Build 1853 on Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/scada/daq_factory_bof.reason: Targets Windows XP and uses a network service on port 20034, making it lab-replicable.

exploits/windows/scada/delta_ia_commgr_b.name: Delta Electronics Delta Industrial Automation COMMGR 1.08 Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/delta_ia_commgr_b.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in Delta Electronics Delta Industrial
          Automation COMMGR 1.08. The vulnerability exists in COMMGR.exe when handling specially
          crafted packets. This module has been tested successfully on Delta Electronics Delta
          Industrial Automation COMMGR 1.08 over
          Windows XP SP3,
          Windows 7 SP1, and
          Windows 8.1.
exploits/windows/scada/delta_ia_commgr_b.reason: Third-party SCADA software can be installed on Windows VMs and exploited remotely without user interaction.

extracted_metadata.platform_notes: Windows XP SP3, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8.1

exploits/windows/scada/diaenergie_sqli.name: DIAEnergie SQL Injection (CVE-2024-4548)
exploits/windows/scada/diaenergie_sqli.description:
SQL injection vulnerability in DIAEnergie <= v1.10 from Delta Electronics.
          This vulnerability can be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to gain arbitrary code execution through a SQL injection vulnerability in the CEBC service. The commands will get executed in the context of NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
exploits/windows/scada/diaenergie_sqli.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software with unauthenticated remote code execution via SQL injection.

exploits/windows/scada/factorylink_csser.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found on Siemens FactoryLink 8. The
          vulnerability occurs when CSService.exe processes a CSMSG_ListFiles_REQ message,
          the user-supplied path first gets converted to ANSI format (CodePage 0), and then
          gets handled by a logging routine where proper bounds checking is not done,
          therefore causing a stack-based buffer overflow, and results arbitrary code execution.
exploits/windows/scada/factorylink_csser.reason: Targets Windows XP SP3 with remote code execution via network service vulnerability.

exploits/windows/scada/factorylink_vrn_0.name: Siemens FactoryLink vrn.exe Opcode 9 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/factorylink_vrn_0.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in FactoryLink 7.5, 7.5 SP2,
          and 8.0.1.703.  By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker may be able to
          execute arbitrary code due to the improper use of a vsprintf() function while
          processing the user-supplied text field.  Originally found and posted by
          Luigi Auriemma.
exploits/windows/scada/factorylink_vrn_0.reason: Targets SCADA software, not standard Windows OS service

exploits/windows/scada/ge_proficy_cimpli.name: GE Proficy CIMPLICITY gefebt.exe Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/scada/ge_proficy_cimpli.description:
This module abuses the gefebt.exe component in GE Proficy CIMPLICITY, reachable through the
        CIMPLICIY CimWebServer. The vulnerable component allows to execute remote BCL files in
        shared resources. An attacker can abuse this behavior to execute a malicious BCL and
        drop an arbitrary EXE. The last one can be executed remotely through the WebView server.
        This module has been tested successfully in GE Proficy CIMPLICITY 7.5 with the embedded
        CimWebServer. This module starts a WebDAV server to provide the malicious BCL files. If
        the target does not have the WebClient service enabled, an external SMB service is necessary.
exploits/windows/scada/ge_proficy_cimpli.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software (GE Proficy CIMPLICITY) and achieves remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/iconics_genbroker.description:
The GenBroker service on port 38080 is affected by three integer overflow
          vulnerabilities while handling opcode 0x4b0, which is caused by abusing the
          the memory allocations needed for the number of elements passed by the client.
          This results unexpected behaviors such as direct registry calls, memory location
          calls, or arbitrary remote code execution.  Please note that in order to ensure
          reliability, this exploit will try to open calc (hidden), inject itself into the
          process, and then open up a shell session.  Also, DEP bypass is supported.
exploits/windows/scada/iconics_genbroker.reason: Targets Windows XP with network service on port 38080, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/scada/iconics_webhmi_se.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in ICONICS WebHMI's ActiveX control.
          By supplying a long string of data to the 'SetActiveXGUID' parameter, GenVersion.dll
          fails to do any proper bounds checking before this input is copied onto the stack,
          which causes a buffer overflow, and results arbitrary code execution under the context
          of the user.
exploits/windows/scada/iconics_webhmi_se.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page and allow the ActiveX control to run.

exploits/windows/scada/igss9_igssdataser.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the igssdataserver.exe component of 7-Technologies
          IGSS up to version 9.00.00 b11063. While processing a ListAll command, the application
          fails to do proper bounds checking before copying data into a small buffer on the stack.
          This causes a buffer overflow and allows to overwrite a structured exception handling record
          on the stack, allowing for unauthenticated remote code execution.  Also, after the payload
          exits, IGSSdataServer.exe should automatically recover.
targets: Windows XP SP3/2003 Server R2 SP2 (DEP Bypass)
exploits/windows/scada/igss9_igssdataser.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software with unauthenticated remote code execution over network.

extracted_metadata.platform_notes: Windows XP SP3/2003 Server R2 SP2

exploits/windows/scada/igss9_igssdataser.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found on 7-Technologies IGSS 9. By supplying
          a long string of data to the 'Rename' (0x02), 'Delete' (0x03), or 'Add' (0x04) command,
          a buffer overflow condition occurs in IGSSdataServer.exe while handing an RMS report,
          which results arbitrary code execution under the context of the user.

          The attack is carried out in three stages.  The first stage sends the final payload to
          IGSSdataServer.exe, which will remain in memory.  The second stage sends the Add command
          so the process can find a valid ID for the Rename command.  The last stage then triggers
          the vulnerability with the Rename command, and uses an egghunter to search for the
          shellcode that we sent in stage 1.  The use of egghunter appears to be necessary due to
          the small buffer size, which cannot even contain our ROP chain and the final payload.
exploits/windows/scada/igss9_igssdataser.reason: Targets Windows XP and exploits a network service without user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/igss9_misc.description:
This module exploits multiple vulnerabilities found on IGSS 9's Data Server and
          Data Collector services.  The initial approach is first by transferring our binary
          with Write packets (opcode 0x0D) via port 12401 (igssdataserver.exe), and then send
          an EXE packet (opcode 0x0A) to port 12397 (dc.exe), which will cause dc.exe to run
          that payload with a CreateProcessA() function as a new thread.
exploits/windows/scada/igss9_misc.reason: Targets Windows XP and achieves remote code execution via network services without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/igss_exec_17.name: Interactive Graphical SCADA System Remote Command Injection
exploits/windows/scada/igss_exec_17.description:
This module abuses a directory traversal flaw in Interactive
          Graphical SCADA System v9.00. In conjunction with the traversal
          flaw, if opcode 0x17 is sent to the dc.exe process, an attacker
          may be able to execute arbitrary system commands.
exploits/windows/scada/igss_exec_17.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software that can be installed in a VM for remote command execution.

exploits/windows/scada/indusoft_webstudi.name: InduSoft Web Studio Arbitrary Upload Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/scada/indusoft_webstudi.description:
This module exploits a lack of authentication and authorization on the InduSoft
          Web Studio Remote Agent, that allows a remote attacker to write arbitrary files to
          the filesystem, by abusing the functions provided by the software.

          The module uses the Windows Management Instrumentation service to execute an
          arbitrary payload on vulnerable installations of InduSoft Web Studio on Windows pre
          Vista. It has been successfully tested on InduSoft Web Studio 6.1 SP6 over Windows
          XP SP3 and Windows 2003 SP2.
exploits/windows/scada/indusoft_webstudi.reason: Remote code execution via file upload to InduSoft Web Studio agent without user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/moxa_mdmtool.name: MOXA Device Manager Tool 2.1 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/moxa_mdmtool.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in MOXA MDM Tool 2.1.
          When sending a specially crafted MDMGw (MDM2_Gateway) response, an
          attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/moxa_mdmtool.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software (MOXA MDM Tool) for remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/mypro_cmdexe.name: mySCADA MyPRO Authenticated Command Injection (CVE-2023-28384)
exploits/windows/scada/mypro_cmdexe.description:
Authenticated Command Injection in MyPRO <= v8.28.0 from mySCADA.
          The vulnerability can be exploited by a remote attacker to inject arbitrary operating system commands which will get executed in the context of NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
exploits/windows/scada/mypro_cmdexe.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software, allows remote authenticated command injection without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/mypro_mgr_cmd.name: mySCADA myPRO Manager Unauthenticated Command Injection (CVE-2024-47407)
exploits/windows/scada/mypro_mgr_cmd.description:
Unauthenticated Command Injection in MyPRO Manager <= v1.2 from mySCADA.
          The vulnerability can be exploited by a remote attacker to inject arbitrary operating system commands which will get executed in the context of the myscada9 administrative user that is automatically added by the product.
exploits/windows/scada/mypro_mgr_cmd.reason: Targets Windows-only SCADA software with network-accessible command injection requiring no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/procyon_core_serv.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the coreservice.exe component of Proycon
          Core Server <= v1.13. While processing a password, the application
          fails to do proper bounds checking before copying data into a small buffer on the stack.
          This causes a buffer overflow and allows to overwrite a structured exception handling
          record on the stack, allowing for unauthenticated remote code execution.  Also, after the
          payload exits, Coreservice.exe should automatically recover.
targets: Windows XP SP3 - No dep bypass
exploits/windows/scada/procyon_core_serv.reason: Remote buffer overflow in Proycon Core Server allows unauthenticated code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/realwin.name: DATAC RealWin SCADA Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/realwin.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in DATAC Control
          International RealWin SCADA Server 2.0 (Build 6.0.10.37).
          By sending a specially crafted FC_INFOTAG/SET_CONTROL packet,
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/realwin.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software and allows remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/realwin_on_fc_bin.name: DATAC RealWin SCADA Server 2 On_FC_CONNECT_FCS_a_FILE Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_on_fc_bin.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in DATAC Control International RealWin
          SCADA Server 2.1 and below. By supplying a specially crafted On_FC_BINFILE_FCS_*FILE
          packet via port 910, RealWin will try to create a file (which would be saved to
          C:\Program Files\DATAC\Real Win\RW-version\filename) by first copying the user-
          supplied filename with an inline memcpy routine without proper bounds checking, which
          results a stack-based buffer overflow, allowing arbitrary remote code execution.

          Tested version: 2.0 (Build 6.1.8.10)
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_on_fc_bin.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software with remote code execution via network packet, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/scada/realwin_on_fcs_lo.name: RealWin SCADA Server DATAC Login Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_on_fcs_lo.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in DATAC Control
          International RealWin SCADA Server 2.1 (Build 6.0.10.10) or
          earlier.  By sending a specially crafted On_FC_CONNECT_FCS_LOGIN
          packet containing a long username, an attacker may be able to
          execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_on_fcs_lo.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software with remote network exploit requiring no user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_init.name: DATAC RealWin SCADA Server SCPC_INITIALIZE Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_init.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in DATAC Control
          International RealWin SCADA Server 2.0 (Build 6.1.8.10).
          By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_init.reason: Targets SCADA software (non-standard Windows service) but can be installed in VM lab

exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_init.name: DATAC RealWin SCADA Server SCPC_INITIALIZE_RF Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_init.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in DATAC Control
          International RealWin SCADA Server 2.0 (Build 6.1.8.10).
          By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_init.reason: Targets SCADA software, not standard Windows OS, and requires third-party SCADA server installation.

exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_txte.name: DATAC RealWin SCADA Server SCPC_TXTEVENT Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_txte.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in DATAC Control
          International RealWin SCADA Server 2.0 (Build 6.1.8.10).
          By sending a specially crafted packet,
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/realwin_scpc_txte.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software that can be installed in a VM for network-based exploitation.

exploits/windows/scada/rockwell_factoryt.name: Rockwell FactoryTalk View SE SCADA Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/scada/rockwell_factoryt.description:
This module exploits a series of vulnerabilities to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution
          on the Rockwell FactoryTalk View SE SCADA product as the IIS user.
          The attack relies on the chaining of five separate vulnerabilities. The first vulnerability is an unauthenticated project copy request,
          the second is a directory traversal, and the third is a race condition. In order to achieve full remote code execution on all
          targets, two information leak vulnerabilities are also abused.
          This exploit was used by the Flashback team (Pedro Ribeiro + Radek Domanski) in Pwn2Own Miami 2020 to win the EWS category.
exploits/windows/scada/rockwell_factoryt.reason: Targets Windows-based SCADA software and achieves unauthenticated remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/scadapro_cmdexe.name: Measuresoft ScadaPro Remote Command Execution
exploits/windows/scada/scadapro_cmdexe.description:
This module allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the
          affected system by abusing via Directory Traversal attack when using the
          'xf' command (execute function). An attacker can execute system() from
          msvcrt.dll to upload a backdoor and gain remote code execution. This
          vulnerability affects version 4.0.0 and earlier.
exploits/windows/scada/scadapro_cmdexe.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software and allows remote command execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/sunway_force_cont.name: Sunway Forcecontrol SNMP NetDBServer.exe Opcode 0x57
exploits/windows/scada/sunway_force_cont.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow found in the SNMP
          NetDBServer service of Sunway Forcecontrol <= 6.1 sp3. The overflow is
          triggered when sending an overly long string to the listening service
          on port 2001.
exploits/windows/scada/sunway_force_cont.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software with network service on port 2001, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/scada/winlog_runtime.name: Sielco Sistemi Winlog Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/winlog_runtime.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Sielco
          Sistem Winlog <= 2.07.00. When sending a specially formatted
          packet to the Runtime.exe service, an attacker may be able to
          execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/scada/winlog_runtime.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software with network-based buffer overflow requiring no user interaction.

exploits/windows/scada/winlog_runtime_2.name: Sielco Sistemi Winlog Buffer Overflow 2.07.14 - 2.07.16
exploits/windows/scada/winlog_runtime_2.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Sielco Sistem Winlog <= 2.07.16.
          When sending a specially formatted packet to the Runtime.exe service on port 46824,
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
targets: Sielco Sistemi Winlog 2.07.14/2.07.16 - Ceramics Kiln Project
exploits/windows/scada/winlog_runtime_2.reason: Targets Windows SCADA software with network service on port 46824, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkbcopyd.name: Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 BKBCopyD.exe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkbcopyd.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000. The vulnerability
          exists in the service BKBCopyD.exe when handling specially crafted packets. This module has
          been tested successfully on Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 R3.08.50 over Windows XP SP3.
targets: Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 R3.08.50 / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkbcopyd.reason: Targets SCADA software on Windows XP, requiring third-party Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 installation.

exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkesimmg.name: Yokogawa CS3000 BKESimmgr.exe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkesimmg.description:
This module exploits an stack based buffer overflow on Yokogawa CS3000. The vulnerability
          exists in the BKESimmgr.exe service when handling specially crafted packets, due to an
          insecure usage of memcpy, using attacker controlled data as the size count. This module
          has been tested successfully in Yokogawa CS3000 R3.08.50 over Windows XP SP3 and Windows
          2003 SP2.
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkesimmg.reason: Targets SCADA software on Windows, requires third-party Yokogawa CS3000 installation

exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkfsim_v.name: Yokogawa CS3000 BKFSim_vhfd.exe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkfsim_v.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow on Yokogawa CS3000. The vulnerability
          exists in the service BKFSim_vhfd.exe when using malicious user-controlled data to create
          logs using functions like vsprintf and memcpy in an insecure way. This module has been
          tested successfully on Yokogawa Centum CS3000 R3.08.50 over Windows XP SP3.
targets: Yokogawa Centum CS3000 R3.08.50 / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkfsim_v.reason: Targets SCADA software on Windows XP, requires third-party Yokogawa CS3000 installation

exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkhodeq_.name: Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 BKHOdeq.exe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkhodeq_.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000. The vulnerability
          exists in the service BKHOdeq.exe when handling specially crafted packets. This module has
          been tested successfully on Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 R3.08.50 over Windows XP SP3 and Windows
          2003 SP2.
exploits/windows/scada/yokogawa_bkhodeq_.reason: Targets SCADA software on Windows but requires third-party Yokogawa CENTUM CS 3000 installation.

exploits/windows/sage/x3_adxsrv_auth_byp.name: Sage X3 Administration Service Authentication Bypass Command Execution
exploits/windows/sage/x3_adxsrv_auth_byp.description:
This module leverages an authentication bypass exploit within Sage X3 AdxSrv's administration
          protocol to execute arbitrary commands as SYSTEM against a Sage X3 Server running an
          available AdxAdmin service.
exploits/windows/sage/x3_adxsrv_auth_byp.reason: Targets Windows-based Sage X3 server software with network-accessible AdxAdmin service for remote SYSTEM command execution.

exploits/windows/rdp/cve_2019_0708_bluek.name: CVE-2019-0708 BlueKeep RDP Remote Windows Kernel Use After Free
exploits/windows/rdp/cve_2019_0708_bluek.description:
The RDP termdd.sys driver improperly handles binds to internal-only channel MS_T120,
          allowing a malformed Disconnect Provider Indication message to cause use-after-free.
          With a controllable data/size remote nonpaged pool spray, an indirect call gadget of
          the freed channel is used to achieve arbitrary code execution.

          Windows 7 SP1 and Windows Server 2008 R2 are the only currently supported targets.

          Windows 7 SP1 should be exploitable in its default configuration, assuming your target
          selection is correctly matched to the system's memory layout.

          HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\TerminalServer\Winstations\RDP-Tcp\fDisableCam
          *needs* to be set to 0 for exploitation to succeed against Windows Server 2008 R2.
          This is a non-standard configuration for normal servers, and the target will crash if
          the aforementioned Registry key is not set!

          If the target is crashing regardless, you will likely need to determine the non-paged
          pool base in kernel memory and set it as the GROOMBASE option.
exploits/windows/rdp/cve_2019_0708_bluek.reason: Targets Windows RDP service remotely without user interaction and can achieve RCE.

extracted_metadata.tested_version: Windows 7 SP1 and Windows Server 2008 R2

exploits/windows/rdp/rdp_doublepulsar_rc.name: RDP DOUBLEPULSAR Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/rdp/rdp_doublepulsar_rc.description:
This module executes a Metasploit payload against the Equation Group's
          DOUBLEPULSAR implant for RDP.

          While this module primarily performs code execution against the implant,
          the "Neutralize implant" target allows you to disable the implant.
exploits/windows/rdp/rdp_doublepulsar_rc.reason: Targets Windows RDP service for remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/proxy/bluecoat_winproxy.name: Blue Coat WinProxy Host Header Overflow
exploits/windows/proxy/bluecoat_winproxy.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the Blue Coat Systems WinProxy
          service by sending a long port value for the Host header in a HTTP
          request.
exploits/windows/proxy/bluecoat_winproxy.reason: Targets Windows proxy software with network-based buffer overflow requiring no user interaction.

exploits/windows/proxy/ccproxy_telnet_pi.name: CCProxy Telnet Proxy Ping Overflow
exploits/windows/proxy/ccproxy_telnet_pi.description:
This module exploits the YoungZSoft CCProxy <= v6.2 suite
          Telnet service. The stack is overwritten when sending an overly
          long address to the 'ping' command.
exploits/windows/proxy/ccproxy_telnet_pi.reason: Targets Windows proxy software with network-based overflow, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/proxy/proxypro_http_get.name: Proxy-Pro Professional GateKeeper 4.7 GET Request Overflow
exploits/windows/proxy/proxypro_http_get.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Proxy-Pro Professional
          GateKeeper 4.7. By sending a long HTTP GET to the default port
          of 3128, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/proxy/proxypro_http_get.reason: Targets Windows proxy software and requires only a network connection to port 3128.

exploits/windows/proxy/qbik_wingate_wwwp.name: Qbik WinGate WWW Proxy Server URL Processing Overflow
exploits/windows/proxy/qbik_wingate_wwwp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Qbik WinGate version
          6.1.1.1077 and earlier. By sending malformed HTTP POST URL to the
          HTTP proxy service on port 80, a remote attacker could overflow
          a buffer and execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/proxy/qbik_wingate_wwwp.reason: Exploits a network service (HTTP proxy) without user interaction and targets Windows.

exploits/windows/postgres/postgres_paylo.name: PostgreSQL for Microsoft Windows Payload Execution
exploits/windows/postgres/postgres_paylo.description:
On default Microsoft Windows installations of PostgreSQL the postgres
          service account may write to the current directory (which is usually
          "C:\Program Files\PostgreSQL\<version>\data" where <version> is the
          major.minor version of PostgreSQL). UDF DLL's may be sourced from
          there as well.

          This module uploads a Windows DLL file via the pg_largeobject method
          of binary injection and creates a UDF (user defined function) from
          that DLL. Because the payload is run from DllMain, it does not need to
          conform to specific Postgres API versions.
exploits/windows/postgres/postgres_paylo.reason: Targets Windows PostgreSQL service and achieves remote code execution via network access to database.

exploits/windows/pop3/seattlelab_pass.name: Seattle Lab Mail 5.5 POP3 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/pop3/seattlelab_pass.description:
There exists an unauthenticated buffer overflow vulnerability
          in the POP3 server of Seattle Lab Mail 5.5 when sending a password
          with excessive length.

          Successful exploitation should not crash either the
          service or the server; however, after initial use the
          port cannot be reused for successive exploitation until
          the service has been restarted. Consider using a command
          execution payload following the bind shell to restart
          the service if you need to reuse the same port.

          The overflow appears to occur in the debugging/error reporting
          section of the slmail.exe executable, and there are multiple
          offsets that will lead to successful exploitation. This exploit
          uses 2606, the offset that creates the smallest overall payload.
