          The other offset is 4654.

          The return address is overwritten with a "jmp esp" call from the
          application library SLMFC.DLL found in %SYSTEM%\system32\. This
          return address works against all version of Windows and service packs.

          The last modification date on the library is dated 06/02/99. Assuming
          that the code where the overflow occurs has not changed in some time,
          prior version of SLMail may also be vulnerable with this exploit. The
          author has not been able to acquire older versions of SLMail for
          testing purposes. Please let us know if you were able to get this
          exploit working against other SLMail versions.
exploits/windows/pop3/seattlelab_pass.reason: Remote unauthenticated buffer overflow in POP3 service that can be reproduced in a VM lab environment.

exploits/windows/persistence/accessibili.name: Accessibility Features (Sticky Keys) Persistence via Debugger Registry Key
exploits/windows/persistence/accessibili.description:
This module makes it possible to apply the 'sticky keys' hack to a session with appropriate
          rights. The hack provides a means to get a SYSTEM shell using UI-level interaction at an RDP
          login screen or via a UAC confirmation dialog. The module modifies the Debug registry setting
          for certain executables.

          The module options allow for this hack to be applied to:

          SETHC   (sethc.exe is invoked when SHIFT is pressed 5 times),
          UTILMAN (Utilman.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+U),
          OSK     (osk.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+U, then launching the on-screen keyboard),
          DISP    (DisplaySwitch.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+P),
          NARRATOR (Narrator.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+CTR+ENTER),
          ATBROKER (AtBroker.exe is invoked by launching accessibility features from the login screen, such as WINDOWS+CTR+ENTER).

          Custom payloads and binaries can be run as part of this exploit, but must be manually uploaded
          to the target prior to running the module.
exploits/windows/persistence/accessibili.reason: Requires only an existing Windows session and registry modification, easily reproducible in a VM lab.

exploits/windows/persistence/assistive_t.name: Assistive Technologies Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/assistive_t.description:
This module achieves persistence by registering a custom Assistive Technology (AT) in the Windows registry.
          Then it configures the system to launch the AT executable during user logon or desktop switch (such as with
          an admin prived program).
          Requires Windows 8 or higher and administrative privileges.
exploits/windows/persistence/assistive_t.reason: Requires only Windows 8+ and admin privileges, no victim interaction or special hardware.

exploits/windows/persistence/image_exec_.name: Windows Silent Process Exit Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/image_exec_.description:
Windows allows you to set up a debug process when a process exits.
          This module uploads a payload and declares that it is the debug
          process to launch when a specified process exits.
exploits/windows/persistence/image_exec_.reason: Targets Windows registry persistence mechanism that can be configured locally in a VM

exploits/windows/persistence/linqpad_des.name: LINQPad Deserialization
exploits/windows/persistence/linqpad_des.description: This module exploits a bug in LIQPad up to version 5.48.00. The bug is only exploitable in paid version of software. The core of a bug is cache file containing deserialized data, which attacker can overwrite with malicious payload. The data gets deserialized every time the app restarts.
exploits/windows/persistence/linqpad_des.reason: Requires victim to use paid LINQPad and restart the app, making it client-side interaction dependent

exploits/windows/persistence/notepadpp_p.name: Notepad++ Plugin Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/notepadpp_p.description: This module create persistence by adding a malicious plugin to Notepad++, as it blindly loads and executes DLL from its plugin directory on startup, meaning that the payload will be executed every time Notepad++ is launched.
exploits/windows/persistence/notepadpp_p.reason: Requires victim to launch Notepad++ to trigger payload execution.

exploits/windows/persistence/registry.name: Windows Registry Only Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/registry.description:
This module will install a payload that is executed during boot.
          It will be executed either at user logon or system startup via the registry
          value in "CurrentVersion\Run" or "RunOnce" (depending on privilege and selected method).
          The payload will be installed completely in registry.
exploits/windows/persistence/registry.reason: Module is for persistence, not initial remote code execution, and requires an existing session to install registry payload.

exploits/windows/persistence/registry_ac.name: Windows Registry Active Setup Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_ac.description:
This module will register a payload to run via the Active Setup mechanism in Windows.
          Active Setup is a Windows feature that runs once per user at login.
          It triggers in a user context, losing privileges from admin to user.

          Active Setup will open a popup box with "Personalized Settings" and the text
          "Setting up personalized settings for: <SETUP_NAME>". However
          this won't occur until the login screen has exited (but before the desktop
          is loaded), and our execution is extremely fast so likely the user will not
          see it.
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_ac.reason: Targets Windows registry persistence mechanism, requires only local access to modify registry.

exploits/windows/persistence/registry_us.name: Windows Registry Persistence via Userinit
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_us.description:
This module will install a payload that is executed during user logon.
          It writes a payload executable to disk and modifies the Userinit registry value
          in "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon" to append the
          payload path, causing it to execute when any user logs in.
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_us.reason: Registry-based persistence that only needs an existing Windows session to set up.

exploits/windows/persistence/service.name: Windows Persistent Service Installer
exploits/windows/persistence/service.description:
This Module will generate and upload an executable to a remote host.
          It will create a new service which will start the payload whenever the service is running. Admin or system
          privilege is required.
exploits/windows/persistence/service.reason: Requires existing admin/system session but can be reproduced in a VM by first obtaining such a session.

exploits/windows/persistence/startup_fol.name: Windows Persistent Startup Folder
exploits/windows/persistence/startup_fol.description:
This module establishes persistence by creating a payload in the user or system startup folder.
          Works on Vista and newer systems.
exploits/windows/persistence/startup_fol.reason: Requires existing local access to place payload in startup folder, not a remote exploit.

exploits/windows/persistence/task_schedu.name: Windows Persistent Task Scheduler
exploits/windows/persistence/task_schedu.description: This module establishes persistence by creating a scheduled task to run a payload.
exploits/windows/persistence/task_schedu.reason: Requires existing local access to create scheduled tasks for persistence

exploits/windows/persistence/wsl/registr.name: Windows WSL via Registry Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wsl/registr.description:
This module will install a payload in WSL and execute it at user
          logon or system startup via the registry value in "CurrentVersion\Run"
          or "RunOnce" (depending on privilege and selected method).
          The payload will be installed completely in registry.

          Staged payloads, like fetch payloads in linux X64 don't tend to work. The payload
          will ask for the stage, then submit the HTTP fetch request
          and when the payload is sent it doesn't execute.

          `cmd/linux/http/x64/meterpreter_reverse_tcp` and unix cmd payloads tend to work.
exploits/windows/persistence/wsl/registr.reason: Targets Windows WSL feature and uses registry for persistence, which can be configured in a VM lab environment.

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Event Log Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter
          that will query the event log for an EVENT_ID_TRIGGER
          (default: failed logon request id 4625) that also contains a specified USERNAME_TRIGGER (note: failed logon auditing
          must be enabled on the target for this method to work, this can be enabled using "auditpol.exe /set /subcategory:Logon
          /failure:Enable"). When these criteria are met a command line event consumer will trigger an encoded powershell payload.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Requires only built-in Windows WMI and event log features, no victim interaction needed

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Interval Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter
          that triggers the payload after the specified CALLBACK_INTERVAL.

          If the persistence is not installed, it will keep triggering payloads to spawn.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Targets Windows and requires existing local access to establish persistence via WMI.

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Process Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter
          that triggers the payload when the specified process is started.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.

          Many built-in apps on Windows 10/11 launch via a modern UWP app (Win32Bridge.Server.exe or ApplicationFrameHost.exe),
          not the legacy binary (like calc.exe). If you pick one of these apps, like calc.exe, it can still be triggered
          from command line, however GUI execution will not work.

          Duplicate CLASSNAMEs will not overwrite, so if the env isn't cleaned up before
          re-exploitation, the exploitation will fail.

          Tested and works being launched from GUI (windows 10):
          chrome.exe (several shells at once)
          calc.exe (only from command line calc.exe or calc)
          msedge.exe (several shells at once)
          cmd.exe
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Targets Windows WMI for persistence and requires existing admin session, making it replicable in a VM lab.

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Logon Timer Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter that
          will trigger the payload after the system has a certain uptime. Payloads will trigger every minute until the set end time.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Targets Windows WMI and requires only local admin privileges, easily set up in a VM.

exploits/windows/oracle/client_system_an.name: Oracle Database Client System Analyzer Arbitrary File Upload
exploits/windows/oracle/client_system_an.description:
This module exploits an arbitrary file upload vulnerability on the Client
          Analyzer component as included in Oracle Database 11g, which allows remote
          attackers to upload and execute arbitrary code. This module has been tested
          successfully on Oracle Database 11g 11.2.0.1.0 on Windows 2003 SP2, where execution
          through the Windows Management Instrumentation service has been used.
exploits/windows/oracle/client_system_an.reason: Targets Windows and allows remote file upload leading to code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/oracle/extjob.name: Oracle Job Scheduler Named Pipe Command Execution
exploits/windows/oracle/extjob.description:
This module exploits the Oracle Job Scheduler to execute arbitrary commands. The Job
          Scheduler is implemented via the component extjob.exe which listens on a named pipe
          called "orcljsex<SID>" and execute arbitrary commands received over this channel via
          CreateProcess(). In order to connect to the Named Pipe remotely, SMB access is required.
          Note that the Job Scheduler is disabled in default installations.
exploits/windows/oracle/extjob.reason: Targets Windows Oracle extjob.exe service via SMB named pipe for remote command execution.

exploits/windows/oracle/osb_ndmp_auth.name: Oracle Secure Backup NDMP_CONNECT_CLIENT_AUTH Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/oracle/osb_ndmp_auth.description:
The module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Oracle Secure Backup.
          When sending a specially crafted NDMP_CONNECT_CLIENT_AUTH packet,
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
targets: Oracle Secure Backup 10.1.0.3 (Windows 2003 SP0/Windows XP SP3)
exploits/windows/oracle/osb_ndmp_auth.reason: Targets Windows and allows remote code execution via network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/oracle/tns_arguments.name: Oracle 8i TNS Listener (ARGUMENTS) Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/oracle/tns_arguments.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Oracle 8i. When
          sending a specially crafted packet containing an overly long
          ARGUMENTS string to the TNS service, an attacker may be able
          to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/oracle/tns_arguments.reason: Targets Windows Oracle TNS service, no victim interaction required, remote code execution possible.

exploits/windows/oracle/tns_auth_sesskey.name: Oracle 10gR2 TNS Listener AUTH_SESSKEY Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/oracle/tns_auth_sesskey.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Oracle. When
          sending a specially crafted packet containing a long AUTH_SESSKEY value
          to the TNS service, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/oracle/tns_auth_sesskey.reason: Exploits a network service vulnerability in Oracle TNS listener on Windows without user interaction.

exploits/windows/oracle/tns_service_name.name: Oracle 8i TNS Listener SERVICE_NAME Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/oracle/tns_service_name.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Oracle. When
          sending a specially crafted packet containing a long SERVICE_NAME
          to the TNS service, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/oracle/tns_service_name.reason: Targets Windows Oracle TNS service for remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/nuuo/nuuo_cms_fu.description:
The COMMITCONFIG verb is used by a CMS client to upload and modify the configuration of the
          CMS Server.
          The vulnerability is in the "FileName" parameter, which accepts directory traversal (..\..\)
          characters. Therefore, this function can be abused to overwrite any files in the installation
          drive of CMS Server.

          This vulnerability is exploitable in CMS versions up to and including v2.4.

          This module will either use a provided session number (which can be guessed with an auxiliary
          module) or attempt to login using a provided username and password - it will also try the
          default credentials if nothing is provided.

          This module will overwrite the LicenseTool.dll file in the CMS Server installation. If the module
          fails to restore LicenseTool.dll then the installation will be corrupted and NCS Server will
          not execute successfully.
exploits/windows/nuuo/nuuo_cms_fu.reason: Targets Windows-based Nuuo CMS Server software with network-accessible vulnerability.

exploits/windows/nuuo/nuuo_cms_sqli.name: Nuuo Central Management Authenticated SQL Server SQLi
exploits/windows/nuuo/nuuo_cms_sqli.description:
The Nuuo Central Management Server allows an authenticated user to query the state of the alarms.
          This functionality can be abused to inject SQL into the query. As SQL Server 2005 Express is
          installed by default, xp_cmdshell can be enabled and abused to achieve code execution.
          This module will either use a provided session number (which can be guessed with an auxiliary
          module) or attempt to login using a provided username and password - it will also try the
          default credentials if nothing is provided.
exploits/windows/nuuo/nuuo_cms_sqli.reason: Targets Windows-based Nuuo CMS software with network SQL injection leading to RCE via xp_cmdshell.

exploits/windows/novell/file_reporter_fs.name: NFR Agent FSFUI Record File Upload RCE
exploits/windows/novell/file_reporter_fs.description:
NFRAgent.exe, a component of Novell File Reporter (NFR), allows remote attackers to upload
          arbitrary files via a directory traversal while handling requests to /FSF/CMD with
          FSFUI records with UICMD 130. This module has been tested successfully against NFR
          Agent 1.0.4.3 (File Reporter 1.0.2) and NFR Agent 1.0.3.22 (File Reporter 1.0.1).
exploits/windows/novell/file_reporter_fs.reason: Targets Windows-based Novell File Reporter service with network-based file upload vulnerability requiring no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/novell/groupwisemesseng.name: Novell GroupWise Messenger Client Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/groupwisemesseng.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Novell's GroupWise Messenger Client.
          By sending a specially crafted HTTP response, an attacker may be able to execute
          arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/novell/groupwisemesseng.reason: Requires victim to use the GroupWise Messenger client to connect to attacker server, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/novell/netiq_pum_eval.name: NetIQ Privileged User Manager 2.3.1 ldapagnt_eval() Remote Perl Code Execution
exploits/windows/novell/netiq_pum_eval.description:
This module abuses a lack of authorization in the NetIQ Privileged User Manager
          service (unifid.exe) to execute arbitrary perl code. The problem exists in the
          ldapagnt module. The module has been tested successfully on NetIQ PUM 2.3.1 over
          Windows 2003 SP2, which allows to execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges.
targets: Windows 2003 SP2 / NetIQ Privileged User Manager 2.3.1
exploits/windows/novell/netiq_pum_eval.reason: Targets Windows with remote code execution via network service without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/novell/nmap_stor.name: Novell NetMail NMAP STOR Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/nmap_stor.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Novell's Netmail 3.52 NMAP STOR
          verb. By sending an overly long string, an attacker can overwrite the
          buffer and control program execution.
exploits/windows/novell/nmap_stor.reason: Targets Windows 2000 Pro with network-based buffer overflow in Novell NetMail service.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_desktop.name: Novell ZENworks 6.5 Desktop/Server Management Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_desktop.description:
This module exploits a heap overflow in the Novell ZENworks
          Desktop Management agent. This vulnerability was discovered
          by Alex Wheeler.
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_desktop.reason: Targets Windows and achieves remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x21 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management 10 SP2. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be
          triggered by sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x21
          (PROXY_CMD_FTP_FILE) to port 998/TCP. The module has been successfully tested on
          Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 and Windows Server 2003 SP2
          (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Remote buffer overflow in Novell ZENworks service on TCP/998 requires no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x4c Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be triggered
          by sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x4c
          (PROXY_CMD_PREBOOT_TASK_INFO2) to port 998/TCP. The module has been successfully
          tested on Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 / SP3 and Windows Server
          2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP3 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Remote buffer overflow in Novell service on TCP/998 requires only third-party software install.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x06 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management 10 SP2. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be
          triggered by sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x06
          (PROXY_CMD_CLEAR_WS) to the 998/TCP port. The module has been successfully tested
          on Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 and Windows Server 2003 SP2
          (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Targets Windows with remote network service on TCP/998, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x6c Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be triggered by
          sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x6c (PROXY_CMD_GET_NEXT_STEP)
          to port 998/TCP. The module has been successfully tested on Novell ZENworks
          Configuration Management 10 SP2 / SP3 and Windows Server 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP3 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Remote buffer overflow in Novell ZENworks service on Windows, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/nntp/ms05_030_nntp.name: MS05-030 Microsoft Outlook Express NNTP Response Parsing Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/nntp/ms05_030_nntp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the news reader of Microsoft
          Outlook Express.
exploits/windows/nntp/ms05_030_nntp.reason: Requires victim to open a malicious NNTP response in Outlook Express (client-side interaction)

exploits/windows/nimsoft/nimcontroller_b.name: CA Unified Infrastructure Management Nimsoft 7.80 - Remote Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/nimsoft/nimcontroller_b.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow within the CA Unified Infrastructure Management nimcontroller.
          The vulnerability occurs in the robot (controller) component when sending a specially crafted directory_list
          probe.

          Technically speaking the target host must also be vulnerable to CVE-2020-8010 in order to reach the
          directory_list probe.
exploits/windows/nimsoft/nimcontroller_b.reason: Targets Windows CA UIM software with network-based buffer overflow requiring no user interaction.

exploits/windows/nfs/xlink_nfsd.name: Omni-NFS Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/nfs/xlink_nfsd.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Xlink Omni-NFS Server 5.2
          When sending a specially crafted nfs packet, an attacker may be able
          to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/nfs/xlink_nfsd.reason: Exploits a network service buffer overflow without user interaction.

exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_mof.name: Oracle MySQL for Microsoft Windows MOF Execution
exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_mof.description:
This module takes advantage of a file privilege misconfiguration problem
          specifically against Windows MySQL servers (due to the use of a .mof file).
          This may result in arbitrary code execution under the context of SYSTEM.
          This module requires a valid MySQL account on the target machine.
targets: MySQL on Windows prior to Vista
exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_mof.reason: Targets Windows MySQL service and achieves SYSTEM-level code execution remotely with valid MySQL credentials.

exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_start_up.name: Oracle MySQL for Microsoft Windows FILE Privilege Abuse
exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_start_up.description:
This module takes advantage of a file privilege misconfiguration problem
          specifically against Windows MySQL servers. This module abuses the FILE
          privilege to write a payload to Microsoft's All Users Start Up directory
          which will execute every time a user logs in. The default All Users Start
          Up directory used by the module is present on Windows 7.
exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_start_up.reason: Targets Windows MySQL servers and achieves remote code execution via FILE privilege abuse without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_yassl_hello.name: MySQL yaSSL SSL Hello Message Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_yassl_hello.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the yaSSL (1.7.5 and earlier)
          implementation bundled with MySQL <= 6.0. By sending a specially crafted
          Hello packet, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/mysql/mysql_yassl_hello.reason: Targets MySQL on Windows with network-based buffer overflow requiring no user interaction.

exploits/windows/mysql/scrutinizer_uploa.description:
This exploits an insecure config found in Scrutinizer NetFlow & sFlow Analyzer.
          By default, the software installs a default password in MySQL, and binds the
          service to "0.0.0.0".  This allows any remote user to login to MySQL, and then
          gain arbitrary remote code execution under the context of 'SYSTEM'.  Examples
          of default credentials include: 'scrutinizer:admin', and 'scrutremote:admin'.
targets: Scrutinizer NetFlow and sFlow Analyzer 9.5.2 or older
exploits/windows/mysql/scrutinizer_uploa.reason: Targets Windows MySQL service with default credentials allowing remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/lyris_listmanager.name: Lyris ListManager MSDE Weak sa Password
exploits/windows/mssql/lyris_listmanager.description:
This module exploits a weak password vulnerability in the
          Lyris ListManager MSDE install. During installation, the 'sa'
          account password is set to 'lminstall'. Once the install
          completes, it is set to 'lyris' followed by the process
          ID of the installer. This module brute forces all possible
          process IDs that would be used by the installer.
exploits/windows/mssql/lyris_listmanager.reason: Targets Windows and achieves remote code execution via network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/ms02_039_slammer.name: MS02-039 Microsoft SQL Server Resolution Overflow
exploits/windows/mssql/ms02_039_slammer.description:
This is an exploit for the SQL Server 2000 resolution
          service buffer overflow. This overflow is triggered by
          sending a udp packet to port 1434 which starts with 0x04 and
          is followed by long string terminating with a colon and a
          number. This module should work against any vulnerable SQL
          Server 2000 or MSDE install (pre-SP3).
exploits/windows/mssql/ms02_039_slammer.reason: Targets Windows SQL Server 2000/MSDE via UDP/1434 without user interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/ms02_056_hello.name: MS02-056 Microsoft SQL Server Hello Overflow
exploits/windows/mssql/ms02_056_hello.description:
By sending malformed data to TCP port 1433, an
          unauthenticated remote attacker could overflow a buffer and
          possibly execute code on the server with SYSTEM level
          privileges. This module should work against any vulnerable
          SQL Server 2000 or MSDE install (< SP3).
exploits/windows/mssql/ms02_056_hello.reason: Remote buffer overflow in SQL Server 2000/MSDE on TCP 1433 needs no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/ms09_004_sp_replw.name: MS09-004 Microsoft SQL Server sp_replwritetovarbin Memory Corruption
exploits/windows/mssql/ms09_004_sp_replw.description:
A heap-based buffer overflow can occur when calling the undocumented
          "sp_replwritetovarbin" extended stored procedure. This vulnerability affects
          all versions of Microsoft SQL Server 2000 and 2005, Windows Internal Database,
          and Microsoft Desktop Engine (MSDE) without the updates supplied in MS09-004.
          Microsoft patched this vulnerability in SP3 for 2005 without any public
          mention.

          An authenticated database session is required to access the vulnerable code.
          That said, it is possible to access the vulnerable code via an SQL injection
          vulnerability.

          This exploit smashes several pointers, as shown below.

          1. pointer to a 32-bit value that is set to 0
          2. pointer to a 32-bit value that is set to a length influenced by the buffer
          length.
          3. pointer to a 32-bit value that is used as a vtable pointer. In MSSQL 2000,
          this value is referenced with a displacement of 0x38. For MSSQL 2005, the
          displacement is 0x10. The address of our buffer is conveniently stored in
          ecx when this instruction is executed.
          4. On MSSQL 2005, an additional vtable ptr is smashed, which is referenced with
          a displacement of 4. This pointer is not used by this exploit.

          This particular exploit replaces the previous dual-method exploit. It uses
          a technique where the value contained in ecx becomes the stack. From there,
          return oriented programming is used to normalize the execution state and
          finally execute the payload via a "jmp esp". All addresses used were found
          within the sqlservr.exe memory space, yielding very reliable code execution
          using only a single query.

          NOTE: The MSSQL server service does not automatically restart by default. That
          said, some exceptions are caught and will not result in terminating the process.
          If the exploit crashes the service prior to hijacking the stack, it won't die.
          Otherwise, it's a goner.
exploits/windows/mssql/ms09_004_sp_replw.reason: Targets Windows SQL Server versions 2000/2005 and requires only an authenticated SQL session, which can be obtained via SQL injection or default credentials.

exploits/windows/mssql/ms09_004_sp_replw.name: MS09-004 Microsoft SQL Server sp_replwritetovarbin Memory Corruption via SQL Injection
exploits/windows/mssql/ms09_004_sp_replw.description:

          This exploit smashes several pointers, as shown below.

          2. pointer to a 32-bit value that is set to a length influenced by the buffer
          length.
          3. pointer to a 32-bit value that is used as a vtable pointer. In MSSQL 2000,

          4. On MSSQL 2005, an additional vtable ptr is smashed, which is referenced with

exploits/windows/mssql/ms09_004_sp_replw.reason: Targets built-in Microsoft SQL Server service on Windows with no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_clr_payload.name: Microsoft SQL Server Clr Stored Procedure Payload Execution
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_clr_payload.description:
This module executes an arbitrary native payload on a Microsoft SQL
          server by loading a custom SQL CLR Assembly into the target SQL
          installation, and calling it directly with a base64-encoded payload.

          The module requires working credentials in order to connect directly to the
          MSSQL Server.

          This method requires the user to have sufficient privileges to install a custom
          SQL CRL DLL, and invoke the custom stored procedure that comes with it.

          This exploit does not leave any binaries on disk.

          Tested on MS SQL Server versions: 2005, 2012, 2016 (all x64).
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_clr_payload.reason: Requires valid SQL credentials but targets Windows SQL Server and achieves remote code execution without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_linkcrawler.name: Microsoft SQL Server Database Link Crawling Command Execution
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_linkcrawler.description:
This module can be used to crawl MS SQL Server database links and deploy
          Metasploit payloads through links configured with sysadmin privileges using a
          valid SQL Server Login.

          If you are attempting to obtain multiple reverse shells using this module we
          recommend setting the "DisablePayloadHandler" advanced option to "true", and setting
          up a exploit/multi/handler to run in the background as a job to support multiple incoming
          shells.

          If you are interested in deploying payloads to specific servers this module also
          supports that functionality via the "DEPLOYLIST" option.

          Currently, the module is capable of delivering payloads to both 32bit and 64bit
          Windows systems via powershell memory injection methods based on Matthew Graeber's
          work. As a result, the target server must have powershell installed. By default,
          all of the crawl information is saved to a CSV formatted log file and MSF loot so
          that the tool can also be used for auditing without deploying payloads.
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_linkcrawler.reason: Requires valid SQL Server credentials but achieves remote code execution over network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_payload.name: Microsoft SQL Server Payload Execution
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_payload.description:
This module executes an arbitrary payload on a Microsoft SQL Server by using
          the "xp_cmdshell" stored procedure. Currently, three delivery methods are supported.

          First, the original method uses Windows 'debug.com'. File size restrictions are
          avoided by incorporating the debug bypass method presented by SecureStat at
          Defcon 17. Since this method invokes ntvdm, it is not available on x64 systems.

          A second method takes advantage of the Command Stager subsystem. This allows using
          various techniques, such as using a TFTP server, to send the executable. By default
          the Command Stager uses 'wcsript.exe' to generate the executable on the target.

          Finally, ReL1K's latest method utilizes PowerShell to transmit and recreate the
          payload on the target.

          NOTE: This module will leave a payload executable on the target system when the
          attack is finished.
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_payload.reason: Targets Windows SQL Server service and achieves remote code execution via xp_cmdshell without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_payload_sql.name: Microsoft SQL Server Payload Execution via SQL Injection
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_payload_sql.description:
This module will execute an arbitrary payload on a Microsoft SQL
          Server, using a SQL injection vulnerability.

          Once a vulnerability is identified this module
          will use xp_cmdshell to upload and execute Metasploit payloads.
          It is necessary to specify the exact point where the SQL injection
          vulnerability happens. For example, given the following injection:

          http://www.example.com/show.asp?id=1;exec xp_cmdshell 'dir';--&cat=electrical

          you would need to set the following path:
          set GET_PATH /showproduct.asp?id=1;[SQLi];--&cat=foobar

          In regard to the payload, unless there is a closed port in the web server,
          you dont want to use any "bind" payload, specially on port 80, as you will
          stop reaching the vulnerable web server host. You want a "reverse" payload, probably to
          your port 80 or to any other outbound port allowed on the firewall.
          For privileged ports execute Metasploit msfconsole as root.

          Currently, three delivery methods are supported.

          First, the original method uses Windows 'debug.com'. File size restrictions are
          avoided by incorporating the debug bypass method presented by SecureStat at
          Defcon 17. Since this method invokes ntvdm, it is not available on x64 systems.

          A second method takes advantage of the Command Stager subsystem. This allows using
          various techniques, such as using a TFTP server, to send the executable. By default
          the Command Stager uses 'wcsript.exe' to generate the executable on the target.

          Finally, ReL1K's latest method utilizes PowerShell to transmit and recreate the
          payload on the target.

          NOTE: This module will leave a payload executable on the target system when the
          attack is finished.
exploits/windows/mssql/mssql_payload_sql.reason: Targets Windows SQL Server via SQL injection for remote payload execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/motorola/timbuktu_fileu.name: Timbuktu Pro Directory Traversal/File Upload
exploits/windows/motorola/timbuktu_fileu.description:
This module exploits a directory traversal vulnerability in Motorola's
          Timbuktu Pro for Windows 8.6.5.
exploits/windows/motorola/timbuktu_fileu.reason: Targets Windows software (Timbuktu Pro) and achieves remote code execution via network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/mmsp/ms10_025_wmss_conn.name: Windows Media Services ConnectFunnel Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/mmsp/ms10_025_wmss_conn.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Windows Media
          Unicast Service version 4.1.0.3930 (NUMS.exe). By sending a specially
          crafted FunnelConnect request, an attacker can execute arbitrary code
          under the "NetShowServices" user account. Windows Media Services 4.1 ships
          with Windows 2000 Server, but is not installed by default.

          NOTE: This service does NOT restart automatically. Successful, as well as
          unsuccessful exploitation attempts will kill the service which prevents
          additional attempts.
exploits/windows/mmsp/ms10_025_wmss_conn.reason: Targets Windows 2000 Server built-in Windows Media Services component for remote code execution.

exploits/windows/misc/achat_bof.name: Achat Unicode SEH Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/achat_bof.description:
This module exploits a Unicode SEH buffer overflow in Achat. By
          sending a crafted message to the default port 9256/UDP, it's possible to overwrite the
          SEH handler. Even when the exploit is reliable, it depends on timing since there are
          two threads overflowing the stack in the same time. This module has been tested on
          Achat v0.150 running on Windows XP SP3 and Windows 7.
targets: Achat beta v0.150 / Windows XP SP3 / Windows 7 SP1
exploits/windows/misc/achat_bof.reason: Targets Windows with network-based buffer overflow on UDP/9256, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/misc/actfax_raw_server_.name: ActFax 5.01 RAW Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/actfax_raw_server_.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in ActFax Server 5.01 RAW server. The RAW
          Server can be used to transfer fax messages without any underlying protocols. To
          note significant fields in the fax being transferred, like the fax number or the
          recipient, ActFax data fields can be used. This module exploits a buffer overflow
          in the handling of the @F506 fields due to the insecure usage of strcpy. This
          module has been tested successfully on ActFax 5.01 over Windows XP SP3 (English).
exploits/windows/misc/actfax_raw_server_.reason: Targets Windows XP SP3 and achieves remote code execution via network without victim interaction.
